title: #eng-security: 4132110000
preview:
Priya Desai: Heads up — we're scheduling retirement of the Legacy Token (v1) used by older mobile SDKs and a handful of partners. Need a short design for a compatibility bridge (shim), telemetry plan, and an escape hatch. Cutting without this will cause auth failures.
Alex Kim: How big is the surface? tenants + traffic numbers?
Priya Desai: Rough numbers: ~12 partner tenants, ~3 mobile apps on SDK <=3.2.1. Peak auth calls ~30k/day; steady ~8–10k/day.
Mateo Alvarez: Proposal: run an auth-bridge sidecar at the edge that accepts the old token, performs a limited exchange to a v2 bearer (server-side) and injects Authorization: Bearer <v2>. Keeps core services unchanged. Alternative: map headers in the proxy but sidecar gives better telemetry and rate-limiting.
Jen Harris: Security notes: the bridge must validate signature+claims, add TTL, and emit audit events (legacy_token=true). We cannot accept unsigned tokens indefinitely. Also require key material in Vault/KMS and strict rotation.
Alex Kim: Rollout idea: feature-flag gated per-tenant: `compat_legacy_token`. Start Canary 1% -> 10% -> 50% -> 100% over 2w. Announce deprecation 90 days before hard cut (document + email + SDK update).
Priya Desai: We'll also ship an SDK helper to call the exchange when it sees an old token: `rw_auth.exchangeLegacyToken()` — try-local-first, fallback to bridge. Push migration to mobile app owners in first week.
Mateo Alvarez: Example nginx+lua snippet to forward legacy token to bridge endpoint: ```
# in edge proxy
set $legacy_token $http_x_legacy_token;
if ($legacy_token) {
  proxy_pass http://auth-bridge.internal/exchange;
  proxy_set_header X-Legacy-Token $legacy_token;
}
```
Jen Harris: Good. Also add escape hatch header `X-RW-Compat-Force: legacy-v1` that bypasses strict reject for known support windows, but only enabled via tenant flag and audited. Add metric `legacy_token.exchange.failures` and alert if >1% of auth volume for 10m.
Alex Kim: Tests: add integration test that uses a malformed legacy token to confirm 401, and a stale-but-signed token to confirm 422 with clear error. Validate metrics emitted: `legacy_token.requests`, `legacy_token.success`, `legacy_token.failures`.
Priya Desai: Docs: deprecation page + migration checklist for partners + SDK migration snippet. Timetable: announce tomorrow, enable canary next Tue, widen over 2w, full deprecate earliest 90 days post-announce.
Mateo Alvarez: Key handling: bridge uses dedicated keypair stored in Vault; exchange endpoint will validate incoming v1 signature against rotated verification keys we publish to partner docs. Rotate bridge keys every 30 days and log rotations.
Jen Harris: Ops: require auditing hooks into Console (tag events with `legacy_token=true`) so SRE/EngSec can query. Add a runbook for rollback: toggle `compat_legacy_token` off per-tenant + global kill-switch.
ops-bot: deploy-bot started canary deployment for `auth-bridge` in edge-us-west-2 at 4132110180.
deploy-bot: canary traffic routing enabled -> 1% -> monitoring enabled.
Alex Kim: Who's owning the partner comms? Priya? Also need a partner acceptance test and a sandbox for them to validate.
Priya Desai: I'll own comms and the sandbox. Action items: 1) Mateo implement bridge + exchange API, 2) Jen add metrics/alerts+runbook, 3) Alex add feature-flag gating + tests, 4) Priya partner comms + SDK helper + docs.
Jen Harris: Also coordinate with legal for any contract language for 90-day deprecation windows.
Mateo Alvarez: I'll raise a PR for the bridge proto and a minimal exchange endpoint tomorrow.
Priya Desai: Thanks all. We'll reconvene after canary metrics look stable. Reaction: :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 3950000001
preview:
Elena Park: quick sync — we need a concrete onboarding plan for KMS/HSM and secrets manager partners who want a marketplace listing + SI integration. two main asks: a technical validation lab (remote) and a simple billing/attribution pattern for deals. can we agree on scope today?
Mateo Alvarez: for the lab, require two HSM access patterns: PKCS#11 for on-prem HSMs and a BYOK HTTP/KMS flow for cloud KMS. must validate key-wrap and unwrap, plus rotation. suggest we publish a minimal test harness (docker-compose) partners can run against our sandbox.
Claire Dubois: security checklist high-level: FIPS mode proof (if available), attestation (vendor-signed), tamper evidence, and deterministic key-wrap vectors. also need a process for handling export-control / crypto licensing questions — legal needs to review.
Sanjay Rao: for the marketplace side, we need metadata for listings: partner logo, support contact, allowed regions, SLA tier, and whether BYOK is supported. attribution: use a referral_code param in checkout + an API webhook to reconcile deals. keeps billing ops simple.
Elena Park: good. on SCIM / SSO: partners must support delta SCIM sync for large orgs (not full re-sync every 24h) and both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated SAML flows for customers that have strict IdP constraints. can we add IdP metadata exchange as a step?
Mateo Alvarez: yes — we'll add a small tool for exchanging SAML metadata (signed XML) and an example `saml-init.sh` script. also include a `scim-delta-sim.py` script for partners to exercise provisioning edge cases.
Claire Dubois: telemetry: require partners to emit these fields in the sandbox report: `keywrap_p95_ms`, `error_rate_5m`, and `provisioning_latency_p95_ms`. we'll provide a dashboard template for the marketplace listing and the acceptance criteria for the validation lab.
Sanjay Rao: commercial ask: we want a simple revenue attribution banding — 90/10 for referrals (low-touch SaaS), 70/30 for SI-led services, and a negotiated co-sell split for enterprise where both teams bring pipeline. also propose $10k co-marketing credit for approved partners that pass the lab. open to feedback.
Elena Park: timeline proposal: publish lab + scripts by next Tue; partners can self-enroll into the sandbox with rate-limited keys; validation window is 2 weeks for turning around tests. after lab pass, we issue a "Validated Connector" badge and start a marketplace listing review (5 business days).
Mateo Alvarez: technical note — require partners to support PKCS#11 slot enumeration and a test vector like:
```
# sample test (pseudo)
openssl rsautl -inkey wrapped.key -decrypt -in secret.bin -out out.bin
# expect fixed 32-byte output for vector 3
```
Sanjay Rao: I'll draft the partner-facing page with listing requirements, timeline, and the referral_code instructions. will include a short FAQ: "what if we only support cloud KMS?" — answer: ok, mark BYOK-cloud only and note restricted regions.
Claire Dubois: legal/compliance: we need to confirm the data processor roles and a minimal DPA addendum for partners that receive customer audit logs. marking legal to review, but recommend we require DPA signed before public listing.
marketplace-bot: created PR #624: partners/marketplace/listings/kms-hsm-validation-lab.md — https://git.redwood.co/pr/624
Elena Park: linking PR. final asks: (1) Mateo — add the `saml-init.sh` and `scim-delta-sim.py` to the repo; (2) Claire — attach the telemetry dashboard JSON + acceptance thresholds; (3) Sanjay — publish the partner page draft and include the attribution webhook schema. target internal review Fri, partner preview next Wed, public listing the following Mon.
Mateo Alvarez: will add scripts today.
Claire Dubois: I'll draft telemetry + attach export-control note for legal.
Sanjay Rao: drafting partner page now. will ping legal about DPA.
Elena Park: thanks all — I'll follow up with a short calendar slot for sign-off and add an "embargo window" checkbox to the partner tracker (48h minimum). :white_check_mark:

title: #sales: 3167890123
preview:
Priya Rao: Heads up — AstraBridge (large healthcare insurer) just escalated. CFO wants a 1pm PT exec call Tuesday to sign off POC, but they flagged two blockers: KMS BYO + SOC2 evidence. Need quick alignment.
Alex Chen: putting together a short runbook. Do we have a current BYO-KMS playbook for Private deploy?
Mateo Alvarez: We have a draft for VPC + BYO-KMS but it assumes Private deployment and on-prem HSM. For Dedicated we'd need legal ok for key escrow.
Sarah Li: I can lead the call demo and cover the latency/cost numbers. Need answers on KMS timeline and a list of SOC2 artifacts they expect.
Rahul Patel: Can you share the questionnaire? I haven't seen it yet. If they want our SOC2 report, we can provide the latest certified PDF. For BYO-KMS we need threat model + attestation from their KMS provider.
Priya Rao: attaching their note from yesterday: "CFO: cannot proceed w/o customer-managed keys for PHI. Security to confirm." They also want a discounted committed rate if we run Dedicated for the POC.
Alex Chen: quick technical note: BYO-KMS path requires 1) network peering + egress allowlist, 2) KMS key wrapping config, 3) operator checklist for key rotation. I can draft the minimal config snippet for the call.
Alex Chen: ```
# minimal KMS binding example
kms_provider: aurakey
key_id: projects/astro/locations/global/keyRings/poc-key
allowlist_ips:
  - 35.203.12.45
  - 34.120.8.16
```
Mateo Alvarez: Platform-wise, we can spin a Private nodepool in their AWS account in ~3 business days if they grant infra role. Dedicated on Redwood side is faster but we can't offer BYO-KMS there without legal changes.
Jordan Bell: Legal here — for escrow/attestation we need their KMS vendor contract copy and ATO details. Also they asked for a short addendum for holdbacks if PHI handling is involved. I can draft a one-pager.
Sarah Li: Pricing ask — CFO pushing for 25% off committed 3-month POC on Dedicated. I need approval or counter to propose on the call.
Priya Rao: Exec ask: can an SVP-level person join the 1pm PT call to reassure them about roadmap + commitments? They said it's a make-or-break signoff.
Rahul Patel: I can put together the SOC2 pack and an email summary of BYO risk mitigations. Will need Ops to confirm we've no data exfil flags.
Alex Chen: @Mateo can you confirm whether we can enable KMS binding in Dedicated as an exception? If yes, slack legal + infra for emergency exception process.
Mateo Alvarez: Not without a contract amendment. I'll escalate to eng-platform lead. ETA for response: EOD today.
Jordan Bell: I'll prepare the addendum draft and circulate for review by noon tomorrow. If someone drops the CFO a quick note that legal is prepping it, that buys time.
OpsBot: [reminder] calendar invite created: "AstraBridge exec POC signoff" Tue 13:00 PT — attendees: Priya Rao, Alex Chen, Sarah Li, Mateo Alvarez, Jordan Bell, Rahul Patel.
Priya Rao: Thanks everyone. Action items: Alex - draft KMS snippet + one-slide risk mitigation (today). Mateo - escalate exception (EOD). Jordan - addendum draft (tomorrow noon). Rahul - SOC2 pack (tomorrow morning). Sarah - pricing counter proposal (today afternoon). I'll ping VP for presence after lunch.
Alex Chen: Working on KMS slide now, will share in channel when done.
Sarah Li: I'll propose counter: 15% off + 50% credits for POC tokens, conditioned on 3-month commit. If legal needs a different phrasing I can adapt.
Mateo Alvarez: FYI: network engineering needs a CIDR to prep peering — @Priya please ask customer for that in the pre-call email.
Priya Rao: Will request CIDR + KMS vendor contact + vendor attestation PDF. Putting a short checklist in the thread now.
Priya Rao: Checklist to ask customer: 1) KMS vendor name + attestation 2) CIDR for peering 3) IAM role for infra 4) Signed NDA if sharing SOC2 5) Decision on Dedicated vs Private for POC
Rahul Patel: Good checklist. I'll prep the SOC2 redaction guidance so we don't overshare.
Jordan Bell: I'll loop in account exec to mention addendum language in pre-call invite.
Priya Rao: Closing note — if eng-platform can't bend on Dedicated BYO-KMS, prioritize Private deploy path for signing. We need to present a clear path on Tuesday. :thumbsup:
Alex Chen: Understood. Will have slide ready by 16:00 PT.
Sarah Li: I'll update the demo to show a Private deployment option and the cost/latency tradeoffs.
Mateo Alvarez: Escalation posted to eng-platform Slack and pinged lead.
Priya Rao: Great — will send pre-call email to AstraBridge in 30m and share this thread in the invite. Thanks team.

title: #eng-platform: 3067890123
preview:
Jin Park: Quick pulse — can lane owners confirm staggered cutover slots for API v2 tomorrow? I want to avoid overlap between /generate-heavy and /embeddings. Proposed windows: 02:00-03:00 UTC (embeddings), 03:00-04:30 UTC (generate-heavy), 04:30-05:00 UTC (light endpoints). Assignments? @Rae @Sasha

Rae Chen: I can take /embeddings slot. Can we move it to 01:45-02:45 UTC instead? Our infra team wants a 15m warmup for cache priming.

Sasha Gomez: I'll own /generate-heavy. 03:00-04:30 works but need a canary at 03:00-03:15 only 1% traffic. @Mateo can you prepare the canary config?

Mateo Alvarez: putting up PR 4872 that toggles flag + adds smoke tests. CI: failing flakily on perf test, investigating. PR: https://git.redwood/infra/4872

ops-bot: Canary deployment started for canary-1 at 2026-03-18T03:00:00Z — status: queued

Jin Park: Thx. @Mateo please post the exact curl commands we'll use to validate canary latency. Also, confirm if SDK needs pinning for customers on old clients.

Mateo Alvarez: curl for canary check:
```
curl -sS -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"endpoint":"/generate-heavy","input":"short"}' https://api.redwood.dev/v2/generate
```
Smoke check: p95 < 450ms for canary. SDK: recommend bumping JS SDK to 1.12.0 to avoid header changes. I'll open SDK PR today.

Sasha Gomez: noted. Can we add fallback route for old clients to v1 for 6 hours if errors spike? Config snippet:
```
route.fallback: {
  on_error: redirect_to: "/v1/",
  max_rate: 5%
}
```

Rae Chen: Sounds good. I'll coordinate with support to tag customers who might need manual migration help. Support contact: @Taylor

Mateo Alvarez: CI green on PR 4872 now. Smoke tests passed locally. Pushed SDK PR 212 in repo redwood-js.

oncall-bot: Deploy slot 03:00 UTC reserved by Jin Park. Pager duty oncall: Rae Chen

Jin Park: Final checklist — owners: /embeddings = Rae, /generate-heavy = Sasha, light endpoints = Mateo. Canary 03:00 UTC 1% for 15m, full ramp 03:15-04:30 if ok. SDK pin announcement drafted. If no objections I'll merge at 23:00 PT today and start the slot.

Sasha Gomez: No objections from me. :thumbsup:

Rae Chen: +1

Mateo Alvarez: +1, will monitor logs and open a rollback if p95 > 700ms

Jin Park: Great — I'll merge and post rollout message to #announcements 30m before cutover. Thanks team.

title: #marketing: 2934567890
preview:
Clara Ito: Quick sync — planning the benchmark launch nurture. Need newsletter + webinar sequence that works across NA/EU/APAC. Key goals: maximize webinar RSVPs, local attendance, and report downloads. Thinking: language-aware segmentation, 3 pre-event emails + 2 post-event followups, plus calendar ICS and timezone-aware reminders.
Mateo Alvarez: I can own segmentation & send windows. Proposed segments: en-US, en-EU (UK/IE), es-ES/LA, ja, and de. Also a separate "customer-high-touch" segment routed via AEs with private RSVP links.
Priya Shah: Copy plan: create compact variants per language and then a brevity experiment — short subject + snappy body vs longer persuasive copy. A/B test copy length within each language on engaged cohorts. For subjects: include local timezone mention when appropriate, e.g., "Live: Benchmark walkthrough — 10am BST / 2am PT".
Greg Novak: ESP notes: dynamic content blocks supported for 5 locales. For any locale not available default to English. Calendar add: include ics and Google Calendar links; watch URL length for some providers. Also ensure encoded UTF-8 subject support for non-Latin scripts.
Deliverability Bot: Deliverability checks: seed tests in localized inboxes (Gmail US, Gmail JP, GMX DE), check feedback loop rates, and monitor for character-set drop issues. Avoid long quoted-printable encoded subjects (>75 chars) which some ISPs truncate. For large sends, throttle per-region to avoid ISP soft-bans.
Clara Ito: Segmentation filters (export-ready) — keep simple:
```
segment_en_us: locale:en-US AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_60d:gte1
segment_ja: locale:ja AND opt_in:newsletter AND engagement_90d:gte1
segment_customer_high: tag:customer AND ae_owner:exists
```
Mateo Alvarez: Send windows (local): NA customers -> Tue 10:00 PT, EU locales -> Wed 11:00 BST, APAC locales -> Thu 09:00 JST. For broad newsletter cohorts send at local morning and schedule throttles: max_chunk=15k, pause=10m.
Priya Shah: Testing plan: within each language run a brevity A/B (Short vs Long) seeded with 10% sample, pick winner at 18h. For subjects also test "calendar-in-subject" vs no-calendar tag on high-touch customers. Track opens -> RSVP -> attendance funnel.
Greg Novak: QA checklist: localized seeds (support@, product@, 1 regional inbox per locale), verify charset in subject for JP/DE, test ICS & Google calendar links, verify personalization tokens in each locale fallback. Also validate List-Unsubscribe header and plain-text fallbacks.
Deliverability Bot: Suppress rules: downloaded_report_45d:true, unsub_product:true, hard_bounce_90d:true, spam_trap_flag:true. For locales with strict consent (DE) double-check legal opt-in fields.
Clara Ito: Ops Bot — create campaign skeleton with locale sub-campaigns and tasks: segments (Mateo), copy briefs & brevity test (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Set dry-run 6 days before primary send.
Ops Bot: Campaign draft created: "Global Benchmark Launch — Multi-locale Newsletter + Webinar Nurture". Tasks added: segmentation (Mateo), copy & tests (Priya), deliverability QA (Greg). Dry-run scheduled 6 days before send.
Mateo Alvarez: Note on metrics: report downloads tracked via utm_campaign=bench_global_launch, utm_medium=email, utm_source=newsletter, utm_content={{locale}}_{{variant}}. For AE links include ae_owner param.
Priya Shah: I'll drop short-long copy variants for en/ja/de/es in the doc today. Keep images lightweight for locales with higher mobile rates.
Greg Novak: Final ops note: run localized seed spam check 48h prior. If any locale seed spam rate >0.35% hold that locale. Also schedule staggered sends by region to smooth SMTP load.
Clara Ito: Perfect — small, focused sends per locale, brevity test, calendar links, and AE private invites. Thanks everyone, dropping initial copy in doc by EOD. :earth_americas:

title: #eng-runtime: 2071112222
preview:
Priya Rao: heads up — saw three multi-gpu NCCL jobs hang in us-west-2b ~00:14 UTC after the TOR config push. jobs were pinned to nodes in rack r12.
Alex Chen: confirmed, started getting alerts from autoscaler + job-controller. affected runs are all 8xA100 p2-style instances. only east-west traffic, intra-pod.
Mateo Alvarez: grabbing logs now. looks like peer QP timeouts followed by qp state stuck in ERROR. attaching quick snippets.
Mateo Alvarez: ```
[ 10.123456] mlx5_core 0000:86:00.0: QP 0x12a3a: fatal error: access violation (retry exceeded)
[ 10.123457] ib_qp_event: QP 0x12a3a reported error state
[ 10.123458] ibv_post_send failed: Invalid argument (22)
```
Jing Wu: did we roll any kernel or driver updates around midnight?
Priya Rao: yes — TOR team pushed a vxlan-offload and TSO/GSO tuning change plus a small bonding driver tweak at 00:05. they said it was a non-disruptive config-only change.
OpsKevin: @PriyaRao can you paste the TOR change? I saw they flipped vxlan offload to 'on' for performance; previous default was off.
Priya Rao: TOR change snippet: ```
etdev: set vxlan_hw_launch=1
nethtool -K vxlan0 hw-tc-offload on
```
Alex Chen: interesting — we usually see issues when HW offloads interact with mlx5 checksum/segmentation offloads. RDMA relies on consistent MTU and non-modified packets for RoCEv2.
Claire Summers: quick repro plan: cordon two nodes in r12, run ib_send_bw and ibv_rc_pingpong to see if raw RDMA flows fail independent of NCCL. Mateo, can you run that?
Mateo Alvarez: ran ibv_rc_pingpong between gpu nodes 12-03 and 12-04. results:
Mateo Alvarez: ```
# ibv_rc_pingpong -d mlx5_0 10.0.12.3 10.0.12.4
Can't get completion event: -110
Qpair stalled after 2s
```
Jing Wu: did you check ethtool -k on the vxlan and phys iface?
Mateo Alvarez: ethtool -k shows TSO/GSO on for eth0 and vxlan0, rx-checksumming on. phys MTU is 9000 but vxlan MTU collapsed to 1500 after the TOR offload change on some paths.
Priya Rao: that MTU mismatch smells like PMTU blackhole with offloads rewriting packets. RoCEv2 uses UDP; fragmentation/encap can break RDMA headers.
SREbot: incident created: INC-2071 — "RoCEv2 qpair stalls after TOR vxlan offload change" and paging on-call.
OpsKevin: temporary mitigation: disable vxlan hw-offload on TORs or force vxlan mtu to 9000 so encapsulated path preserves headroom. Which is faster?
Claire Summers: fastest is to flip back TOR vxlan-offload to previous state — roll back and watch. If not possible quickly, we can disable TSO/GSO on host veth/vxlan interfaces as a host-side band-aid.
Alex Chen: host commands to try on affected nodes:```
# disable offloads on vxlan interface
ethtool -K vxlan0 gso off gro off tso off txvlan off rxvlan off
# ensure vxlan mtu
ip link set dev vxlan0 mtu 9000
```
Mateo Alvarez: applied host mitigations on two nodes (disabled gso/gro/tso on vxlan0). re-ran ibv_rc_pingpong and ib_send_bw: now clean, no qpair stalls.
Mateo Alvarez: ```
# ibv_rc_pingpong -d mlx5_0 10.0.12.3 10.0.12.4
bytes 1: 4096  4096  1.0 usec avg
```
Priya Rao: can we get TORs rolled back? OpsKevin coordinating with TOR team now.
OpsKevin: TORs rolled back to previous vxlan offload setting at 00:42 UTC for r12. Monitoring for 10m.
Jing Wu: follow-ups: add a test in our preflight that exercises RoCEv2 path after any TOR offload change; add a guard that rejects vxlan mtu < 9000 for GPU racks.
Claire Summers: also add a runbook step: if RDMA qpair stalls show up, check vxlan/phys MTU and host offloads (gro/gso/tso).
Priya Rao: summary: root cause = vxlan hw-offload + TOR cfg change causing vxlan MTU/segmentation mismatch leading to encapsulation fragmentation and RDMA QP stalls. host-side mitigation (disable GSO/GRO/TSO on vxlan) restored connectivity; TOR rollback permanent fix.
Alex Chen: filing a small RFC to add automated validation for RDMA-sensitive offload changes. cc: @networking @eng-infra
SREbot: INC-2071 status -> resolved (mitigation applied + TOR rollback).

title: #eng-runtime: 2003456789
preview:
Priya Patel: Heads up — our embedding websocket blackbox in asia-south1 started throwing transient 5xx + handshake timeouts around 03:10 UTC. SLO alert for p95 latency fired. Anyone seeing cluster/backplane signals?

probe-bot: Alert: probe/emb-ws-asia-south1 increased error_rate > 3% over 5m. Probe rate=1/s. Sample payload size=512 tokens.

Mateo Alvarez: I saw a small spike in pod restarts on the gpu-pool in that region at the same time. CPU on the sidecars ~80% for short bursts.

Liu Chen: Noticing a lot of short-lived SNAT allocations in the NAT gateway metrics — ephemeral port reuse went up. Could be ephemeral port exhaustion causing handshake failures (we saw this pattern before).

Ava Kim: Is the synthetic probe sampling rate the same across all regions? Cost-wise we run 1/s in asia-south1 because it's a high-traffic sim, but that might be overkill during low-traffic hours.

Priya Patel: Yes it's currently 1/s. This thread is mainly to decide if it's a noisy SLO (probe-induced) vs real capacity issue. Two asks: 1) correlate probe errors with actual user traffic for embeddings; 2) tune probes/alerts so they don't create false positives and cost blowups.

Mateo Alvarez: Quick correlate: user embeddings QPS during the spike was flat (no user-side increase). So more likely probe noise or infra-wide transient.

Liu Chen: Proposed immediate mitigations: reduce probe rate to 1/10s for asia-south1 during the 00-08 UTC window, enable connection pooling on the probe runner side, and increase the prometheus alert window from 5m to 10m to reduce flapping alerts.

Ava Kim: Agree on rate reduction. We should also adjust the alert rule to use a rolling quantile/histogram to avoid single-bucket bursts triggering SLOs.

Priya Patel: Can someone propose concrete prometheus rule/Dashboard changes? I want minimal risk and clear rollback.

Liu Chen: Draft rule snippet (quick):

```
# use avg over 10m and require sustained error ratio
- alert: EmbeddingsProbeHighErrorSustained
  expr: rate(probe_errors_total{job="emb-ws", region="asia-south1"}[10m]) / rate(probe_requests_total{job="emb-ws", region="asia-south1"}[10m]) > 0.02
  for: 10m
  labels:
    severity: page
  annotations:
    summary: "Sustained embed probe errors in asia-south1"
```

Mateo Alvarez: Also add `ignore_common_noise` label for bot-originated probes so dashboards don't aggregate them with user-facing checks.

ops-bot: Note: adjusting probe rate will reduce synthetic billing; estimate ~65% saving in asia-south1 probe spend for the night window.

Priya Patel: Action items: Liu to roll the prom rule + change scrape/alert windows (canary in staging), Mateo to throttle probe runners (change from 1/s -> 0.1/s) for the quiet hours and enable pooling, Ava to update Grafana panels to use histogram_quantile(0.95, ...) and add a "synthetic-only" series. I'll monitor the alerts for 6 hours and confirm.

Ava Kim: Quick ops note — for dashboard smoothing use `increase()` on counters with `offset` windows for noise immunity. I'll add a small panel description explaining why thresholds are higher for Asia.

Mateo Alvarez: Rolling the probe change now to a 10% sample and enabling keepalive pooling. Command I used on the probe-runner k8s job: 

```
kubectl -n infra set env job/probe-runner SAMPLE_RATE=0.1 KEEPALIVE=true && kubectl rollout restart job/probe-runner -n infra
```

Liu Chen: I've created a PR for the alert rule with the `for: 10m` and region scoping. Linking in case reviewers want to tweak: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra-monitoring/pull/482

Priya Patel: Thanks team. Reopening if this recurs after the changes. If we still see errors with reduced synthetic load, escalate to infra-networking + cloud provider for NAT scaling.

ops-bot: Reminder: cost impact estimate saved to ticket INF-2784.

Ava Kim: Will add a small note to the SLO runbook about probe sampling and cost/false-positive tradeoffs. :white_check_mark: 

Priya Patel: Closing for now — will follow up with a short postmortem if we need deeper action. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1972500000
preview:
Elena Voss: quick follow-up from the postmortem  we listed remediation items but didn't lock owners for runbook/playbook edits and recurrence tracking. can folks volunteer?
Harper Lee: I can own the triage steps + safe-check snippets. target: Fri EOD.
Mateo Alvarez: I'll take the escalation matrix and oncall paging thresholds. aim for next Wed.
Jin Park: I can add recurrence tagging automation & a saved query, plus a short playbook section. target: Tue.
Elena Voss: great. please paste file paths and commit dates so I can update the action tracker. also include a tiny eval in PRs (MTTI before vs after).
Harper Lee: triage runbook will include idempotent commands and a verification checklist. sample:
```
# get p95 for model
rwinfctl metrics query --model=chat-main --window=5m --stat=p95
# dry-run route switch
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100 --dry-run
# apply when confirmed (idempotent)
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100
```
Mateo Alvarez: paging change proposal: extend transient p95 window from 5m -> 10m for alerts, add step: "confirm spike not from scheduled batch job" before paging. I'll annotate staging vs prod.
Jin Park: recurrence tagging: label recurrence=yes and field recurrence_count. saved filter: "recurrence:yes AND created>=-30d". will add grafana tile for "recurring incidents" and link in playbook.
ops-bot: created TKT-9988 (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/TKT-9988) "Runbook & Playbook edits from 1972499990 outage" assigned to Harper, Mateo, Jin. Due dates set per thread.
sre-oncall: noted. will test threshold changes in canary and add a dashboard panel for recurrence count.
Harper Lee: runbook path: docs/runbooks/triage/latency-triage-v2.md
Mateo Alvarez: escalation doc: docs/runbooks/escalation/paging-matrix-v3.md (diagram in drawio attached in PR)
Jin Park: recurrence playbook: docs/playbooks/recurrence/recurrence-ops.md and infra/grafana/dashboards/recurrence-count.json
Elena Voss: thanks. once PRs are opened drop links here and include the tiny eval plan. I'll close the postmortem actions after merges.
Mateo Alvarez: will include a short checklist for oncall to run before escalating.
Harper Lee: opening draft PR now, will include test plan + MTTI check.
Jin Park: invite sent for biweekly "recurrence review" sync and added a short notes template in the playbook.
Elena Voss: perfect. closing loop: Harper (Fri), Jin (Tue), Mateo (Wed). pinging for reviews when PRs are ready. thanks everyone. :heart:

# get p95 for model

# dry-run route switch

# apply when confirmed (idempotent)
rwinfctl router set-route --route=chat-main --model=chat-fallback --weight=100
```
Mateo Alvarez: paging change proposal: extend transient p95 window from 5m -> 10m for alerts, add step: "confirm spike not from scheduled batch job" before paging. I'll annotate staging vs prod.
Jin Park: recurrence tagging: label recurrence=yes and field recurrence_count. saved filter: "recurrence:yes AND created>=-30d". will add grafana tile for "recurring incidents" and link in playbook.
ops-bot: created TKT-9988 (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/TKT-9988) "Runbook & Playbook edits from 1972499990 outage" assigned to Harper, Mateo, Jin. Due dates set per thread.
sre-oncall: noted. will test threshold changes in canary and add a dashboard panel for recurrence count.

Mateo Alvarez: escalation doc: docs/runbooks/escalation/paging-matrix-v3.md (diagram in drawio attached in PR)

Jin Park: invite sent for biweekly "recurrence review" sync and added a short notes template in the playbook.

title: #eng-runtime: 1967890123
preview:
Alex Chen: Heads up — seeing a TTFT spike on mixed int4+fp8 runs for long-prefill workloads on rtx-pool. Numbers from yesterday's smoke are attached below, looks like the dequant hotpath is getting invoked more during prefill for long contexts.

Priya Rao: Ugh. Is this the prefill-only profile or end-to-end? Any chance kv-eviction frequency changed?

Alex Chen: Prefill-heavy. End-to-end shows TTFT jump even though TPS is stable. KV eviction metrics are normal. Sending the pprof slice.

Mateo Alvarez: paste profiling snippet:
```
# bench: longprefill_mixcalib (1000 tokens)
prefill_total_ms: 3620
  - matmul_kernels_ms: 2100
  - kv_dequant_ms: 840
  - kv_copy_ms: 180
decode_total_ms: 1850
  - matmul_kernels_ms: 1200
  - kv_dequant_ms: 120
  - overhead_ms: 530
tokens: 1000
TTFT_ms_per_token_prefill: 3.62
TTFT_ms_per_token_decode: 1.85
TPS_prefill: 276
TPS_decode: 540
# baseline (no mixed calib): prefill 2.1ms/token, decode 1.9ms/token
```

Jun Park: Interesting — baseline had prefill faster than decode, now prefill is slower. That 840ms kv_dequant is suspicious. Might be dequant happening eagerly during prefill because of calibration fallback.

Grace Liu: Could this be the new kernel-selection heuristic picking fp8 matmul + int4 kv dequant path for certain layers? We changed the score func last week to prefer lower memory.

Alex Chen: Exactly my thought. I toggled kernel-selection to prefer stable int8 kernels for layers > 32 and re-ran: prefill dropped to 2.05ms/token, decode ~1.95ms/token. But TPS dropped on mixed short requests.

Mateo Alvarez: Quick micro-batching tweak that might help (from bench):
```json
{"batching": {"microbatch_size": 4, "max_latency_ms": 12, "kv_merge_threshold": 256}}
```
Apply kv_merge_threshold to delay dequant until batch assembly — reduces redundant dequant calls.

Priya Rao: Nice. Any numbers after that change?

Alex Chen: After kv_merge_threshold tweak: prefill 2.3ms/token, decode 1.9ms/token, TTFT improvement ~36% vs worst-case. TPS down ~8% for small 1-2 token requests.

Jun Park: Tradeoff makes sense — TTFT vs TPS. For long-context generation, prefer lower TTFT; for high-throughput short completions prefer lower per-request overhead. We should expose policy flag to route by route.

Grace Liu: Agree. Also add an experiment: lazy_dequant_on_prefill=true (dequant only at decode) vs eager_dequant=true. Could be route-configurable.

Mateo Alvarez: I ran lazy_dequant_on_prefill=true experiment overnight on 10 nodes:
```
- observed: prefill_total_ms: 2100 (2.1ms/token), decode_total_ms: 2200 (2.2ms/token)
- notes: decode spikes for first token due to on-demand dequant, but amortized decode ok for long outputs
- regression windows: small requests (<8 tokens) saw 20% worse p99 latency
```

Alex Chen: TL;DR: two knobs: kernel-selection scoring (favor stable kernels for long layers) + batching/kv_merge heuristics. Combined gives ~30% TTFT improvement for long-prefill quantized runs but costs 5-10% TPS on short requests.

Priya Rao: Can we have the console show estimated TTFT vs TPS tradeoff per route so SRE/product can pick defaults?

Grace Liu: I'll add a small dashboard card and a rollout flag. Would like to avoid global default change until we validate on customer traces.

perf-bot: benchmark job 1967890123-mixcalib-sampling completed — full report uploaded to /s3/perf/reports/1967890123-mixcalib.json :thumbsup:

Alex Chen: Thanks — next steps: 1) PR to kernel score tweak (Jun + Mateo), 2) feature flag for kv_merge_threshold (Grace), 3) perf run on customer-like trace (Priya). I'll open tasks and link here.

Priya Rao: I'll queue the trace run and validate p99 impact across routes.

Jun Park: I'll draft the kernel-score change and add unit coverage for selection heuristics.

Grace Liu: I'll wire the flag + dashboard card. We'll gate rollout behind perf acceptance. :white_check_mark:

title: #product: 1955554321
preview:
Olivia Chen: Quick design review — compare table in compact mode + performance badges. Need accessibility + copy checks before we ship the density option. Figma: https://www.figma.com/file/xxxxx (compact-compare frames)
Raj Patel: pushed an update to the compact frames. pin icon is smaller, but it now sits very close to the badge. feels cramped.
Zoe Kim: noticed keyboard focus order is wrong in dense rows — you can tab into the row but the pin button gets skipped.
Priya Singh: accessibility note — VoiceOver reads the badge as just 'badge'. We should expose a descriptive aria-label with metric + unit + intent, e.g. 'Performance: 120 requests per second, p95 12 milliseconds'.
Dan Rivera: also seeing layout shift when badges render asynchronously — on low bandwidth the badge loads after text and pushes content. causes a janky experience.
Emma Liu: can we use a fixed-width skeleton for badges to avoid layout shifts? even a placeholder dot of the same size would help.
Raj Patel: agreed on skeleton. Copy question: current tooltip says 'Latency: 12ms — Good'. that's vague. propose 'P95 latency 12ms — within SLAs'. shorter alt: 'P95 12ms'.
Priya Singh: for screen readers I'd prefer a full sentence in aria-label and a short label for sighted users. example suggestion (aria):
```aria-label="Performance: 120 requests per second; p95 latency 12 milliseconds; meets SLO."```
Zoe Kim: implementation note — we'll wire aria-label to the badge element and keep the visible tooltip for sighted users. need to check focusable order for keyboard users (badge shouldn't steal tab unless actionable).
Mateo Alvarez: one more UX nit: when you pin a version the row height grows due to an added row of metadata. in compact mode that collapses nearby rows visually. maybe show a small inline pill instead of full row expand.
Olivia Chen: good point — prefer inline pill. can we mock that in Figma and test with real data?
Raj Patel: i'll add a variant: inline pill + compact spacing. will also try a slightly bigger hit target for pin so it's keyboard friendly despite density.
Priya Singh: please also add aria-hidden on purely decorative badges and ensure role="img" with alt text where content conveys meaning. I can draft a short acceptance checklist for a11y.
Dan Rivera: will add CSS to reserve space for badge (width: 84px) and implement lazy placeholder. Zoe, can you coordinate the focus order fix in the same PR?
Zoe Kim: yep, i'll take the PR. quick question on testing — do we have a runner for VO smoke tests on the catalog pages?
Emma Liu: not automated yet. I can run manual voiceover checks and dump notes in the Figma comment.
Olivia Chen: timeline? want this ready for rollout on Tuesday's deploy.
Raj Patel: i'll upload updated frames by EOD today.
Zoe Kim: aim to open a PR by tomorrow morning.
Priya Singh: i'll produce the a11y checklist and run a quick validation after the PR lands.
Olivia Chen: thanks team — minor copy tweak task for me: shorten badge tooltip to 'P95 12ms — within SLO' and leave the detailed aria-label. thumbs? :thumbsup::thumbsdown:
Emma Liu: :thumbsup:
Mateo Alvarez: :thumbsup:
Dan Rivera: :thumbsup:
Priya Singh: :thumbsup:
Zoe Kim: :thumbsup:
Raj Patel: :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1955009999
preview:
Priya Shah: Heads-up: multiple EU customers reporting spikes in latency and auth failures since ~09:10 UTC, forwarded from support. They say requests are routing to APAC nodes (200+ ms to 5000+ ms).
Dan Li: I'm seeing an automated change from routing-map service at 09:08 UTC — a label rename for `eu-west-v2` -> `eu-west-2` that shouldn't have happened. Could that have broken region matching?
Mateo Alvarez: Logs show fallback policy kicked in due to a route mismatch. Sample trace: `req=abc123 region=eu-west expected_host=eu-west-1 redir=ap-sg-1 latency=2.1s auth_status=401`
Priya Shah: Customers also mention intermittent 401s and duplicate billing events for a few requests (CS raised billing alarms). Not all customers affected, seems scoped to certain prefixes.
OpsBot: ALERT: routing-map-service anomaly detected: 09:07:44 config-update user=infra-bot change=label-rename routing-map/v1
Elena R.: Confirmed — the label rename triggered the fallback rule that prefers any matching region name; when `eu-west-v2` didn't match, fallback matched `*-west` pattern and selected APAC pool due to ordering bug.
Dan Li: Quick repro for devs: `curl -H "Host: api.redwood.com" -H "X-Region: eu-west-v2" https://api.redwood.com/v1/generate` — observe `x-served-region: ap-sg-1` and `x-token-check: kms-region-mismatch`.
Mateo Alvarez: Repro logs attached in the ticket (pasted):
```
REQUEST id=abc123 headers={X-Region: eu-west-v2}
ROUTER match=label_name==eu-west-v2 -> no match
ROUTER fallback=pattern=*west -> matched ap-sg-1
INFER runtime=ap-sg-1 auth=kms-eu -> auth fail KMS region mismatch
```
Sara Kim: Support: we've told impacted customers we're investigating and provided temporary workaround (set `X-Region` to `eu-west-1` if possible). A few enterprise customers want a timeline.
Priya Shah: Immediate mitigation performed at 09:25 UTC: rolled back label rename and applied a strict-match hotfix to routing policy. Traffic normalization starting ~09:28 UTC.
Dan Li: Mitigations we applied: 1) rollback config 2) hotpatch to require exact label equality before pattern fallback 3) temporarily disable automated label-sync from infra-bot.
Mateo Alvarez: Confirmed post-rollback traces show requests served by eu-west pools again and auth handshakes succeeding. Sample: `req=def456 x-served-region=eu-west-1 auth_status=200 latency=120ms`
Priya Shah: Support update: majority of affected customers report restored service. Two enterprise accounts still saw delayed token charges; billing team is investigating root cause of duplicates.
Elena R.: Root cause summary: an automated label-sync job renamed `eu-west-v2` to `eu-west-2` (typo) and our router's fallback matcher had a precedence bug that allowed broad pattern matches to prefer APAC in absence of exact label. This created cross-region routing and KMS region mismatches -> 401s and elevated latency.
Dan Li: Why did label-sync rename happen? It was a downstream normalization script for region aliases. The script applies transforms without validating against the active routing map.
Sara Kim: CS note: customers want assurance this won't recur. We need a customer-friendly postmortem and a list of compensations for billing anomalies.
Priya Shah: Action items (owners):
- Dan: add validation to label-sync to check active routing map before renames + test (ETA 48h)
- Elena: add unit + e2e test for router precedence (ETA 72h)
- Mateo: create alert for KMS region mismatch spikes (ETA 24h)
- Finance/Sara: reconcile billing duplicates and propose credits (ETA 72h)
- Ops: reintroduce automated label-sync behind canary and gating (ETA 1 week)
Dan Li: Temporary safeguards: revert automation to manual approval; I pushed a gate requiring human confirm for region label changes.
Mateo Alvarez: Postmortem draft suggestions: include timeline, root cause (label-sync typo + router precedence bug), impact (latency, 401s, billing dupes), mitigations, long-term fixes, and customer communication.
Priya Shah: I'll draft initial customer-facing note and the internal postmortem. Draft ETA 4 hours, circulating for review.
SupportBot: Ticket update created: INC-2026 linked to this thread. Impacted customers: list attached.
Elena R.: Also recommend: add a canary that verifies KMS handshake region before accepting routing-map changes.
Sara Kim: I'll prepare SLAs and proposed compensations; want approval from finance before sending.
Dan Li: After hotfix, deploy pipeline will include a static analysis check that flags renames which change canonical region tokens.
Priya Shah: Thanks team. I'll post postmortem link in this thread when ready and coordinate customer comms with Sara/Finance.
Elena R.: Temporary retrospective note — we had a single point where human-readable label normalization could alter programmatic identifiers. We'll treat labels as immutable identifiers going forward.
Priya Shah: Closing updates: service restored fully by 09:30 UTC, monitoring for 48h, follow-ups scheduled.
Dan Li: I'll add the issue to the infra backlog and tag for high priority.
Mateo Alvarez: Adding remedial test to CI now.
Priya Shah: Great work on rapid rollback. We'll sync tomorrow 10:00 UTC for a quick runthrough before the postmortem goes public. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-runtime: 1876543210
preview:
Noah Reed: Quick post-RFC sync: proposing two coordinated changes to reduce host fragmentation and cold-page cost: 1) dynamic slab partitioning that adjusts per-tenant slab budgets based on hotness and recent residency, 2) a compressed cold-tier on local NVMe for evicted pages (async compress/decompress). Wants to capture tradeoffs before a PR.

Sana Kaur: High-level +1. Questions: how do we avoid CPU compression starving inference? How do we handle rewarm latency when a compressed page is needed synchronously?

Mateo Alvarez: From the prototype: we used zstd-level 3 for compress on-evict (fast) and a small async compressor pool. Benchmarks (canary host A100 80GB + NVMe):
```
compress level=3: ratio ~3.6x, compress cpu ~2.5ms/page, decompress cpu ~0.9ms/page
median rewarm (with async prefetch): +3% p50, p95: +11% when decompression happens on-path
with prefetch-on-evict-hint (keep last 30s touched pages cached): p95 impact ~+2%
NVMe read latency under load: baseline 1.8ms -> 7-9ms at high concurrency
```
So NVMe and decompression both add tail; prefetch + rate-limits are key.

Noah Reed: Exactly the concerns. I'm thinking: make compression async on eviction; store metadata in ephemeral in-memory index. When an access misses resident memory but finds compressed cold copy, we issue an async fetch+decompress and return a short 202-like signal to client via eviction-notify / rewarm-suggestion so SDKs can retry (or the runtime can block-wait bounded by SLO). UX options: transparent sync decompress vs. cooperative retry.

Sana Kaur: Cooperative retry is nicer for tail control, but harder on SDKs. Could we provide both: runtime-block-policy: {block_sync_ms=50|never}, and SDKs can opt-in to non-blocking. Also need admission control so compression tasks don't saturate CPU.

Mateo Alvarez: Implementation notes: compressor pool sized = min(cores/4, 8) by default; NVMe IO window limited by aio_tokens. Decompression is cheap but still non-zero; if we block on-path we need to cap parallel decompressions to protect GPU threads. We also saw pathological case where many cold pages were requested at once causing NVMe queue storm and p99 blowup. Solution: per-host NVMe queue limiter + per-tenant cold_fetch_budget.

Noah Reed: For fragmentation: dynamic slab partitioning should shrink slab footprints for tenants that demote frequently and inflate for tenants with sustained hotness. Evictor assigns slab slices atomically using a moving-average hotness score. When a slab slice is reclaimed we attempt to coalesce neighbor free buckets. We avoid full compaction except during long idle windows.

Sana Kaur: Important to surface metrics: slab_slice_utilization, per-tenant cold_bytes, compress_ratio, cold_fetch_latency, compressor_pool_util. Add alert: compressor_pool_util>0.8 && cold_fetch_latency_p95>50ms -> throttle compress or reduce canary.

Mateo Alvarez: Also need a correctness story for pinned pages (e.g., models marking pages non-evictable). Pinned pages bypass slab resizing and compression; but we must tie pinned counts to tenant quotas and disallow overcommit. Pinning will be limited behind a tenant quota and FF.

Noah Reed: API sketch for ops/SDKs:
```
// server-side config
set_cold_tier_policy(tenant, compress=true, compression_level=3, compressible=true)
// runtime hint from SDK
eviction_hint(key, prefer_cold=true)
// per-tenant knobs via console
tenant_config.tenant_x.cold_fetch_budget = 10/sec
```
We should avoid surface for basic users; defaults should auto-enable compression for large pages > 64KB.

Sana Kaur: Rollout plan: land dynamic-slab behind FF memory.dynamic_slab=false and cold-tier behind memory.cold_tier=false. Initial canary: 1% hosts with compressed cold-tier read-only (no on-path decompression; runtime returns rewarm suggestion). Then canary 2: allow bounded on-path decompression with block_sync_ms=30 and strict compressor pool limits. Gate on p95, p99, NVMe queue depth, and compressor CPU util.

Mateo Alvarez: I'll add simulations with Q4_replay + spike tests (simultaneous rewarm storm) and tune per-tenant cold_fetch_budget. Also add a backpressure mode where cold fetches are dropped and SDKs receive preemptive eviction-notify with suggested_jittered_backoff.

infra-bot: feature-flag memory.dynamic_slab_compression created. Canary scheduled: group=cold-tier-canary start=Sun 04:00 UTC. Dashboards: /d/memory/cold-tier-compression (owner: @Mateo).

Noah Reed: Action items: Mateo run sims + add NVMe limiter; Sana write RFC appendix on operator metrics and FF gates; I'll draft SDK guidance for cooperative retry patterns and sample code. Keep pinned/sticky disabled until we have quota enforcement. Ping thread when sims land. :+1:

Mateo Alvarez: Will post sim links and perf graphs here by EOD tomorrow.

Sana Kaur: +1

infra-bot: canary created (id: canary-2026-11-18-07).

title: #eng-runtime: 1870001111
preview:
Sam Nguyen: heads up — after yesterday's kernel-selector tweak we're seeing a P50 token latency regression on /chat/large. Not huge but reproducible in canary and in a subset of prod requests. dashboard: https://graf.redwood.internal/d/uxr3k/multi-route-overview

Lee Ortega: saw that too. SM occupancy dropped on slice-2 and slice-4 for the affected hosts, VRAM usage nudged up ~200MB, and power draw pulses when the batcher kicks. linking a quick screenshot: https://img.redwood.internal/s/shot123.png :thinking_face:

Priya Rao: metric snippets I pulled from prometheus this morning:```
# avg token latency by route
sum(rate(token_latency_seconds_sum{route="/chat/large"}[5m])) / sum(rate(token_latency_seconds_count{route="/chat/large"}[5m]))
# sm occupancy by MIG slice
avg(nvidia_smi_sm_occupancy{mig_slice=~"slice-[2|4]"})
```

Mateo Alvarez: possible root cause: kernel-selector started preferring single-stream kernels for certain sequence-length distributions, which increases VRAM working set and prevents effective KV reuse for batched prefixes. Essentially more eviction churn in the KV cache.

Sam Nguyen: can we correlate KV cache evictions with request traces? I saw increased eviction_rate on the same hosts. If eviction spikes right before token latency spikes, that's our smoking gun.

Lee Ortega: I dumped a small tail sample from canary host-17:```
EVICT key=ctx-482 len=2048 age=32ms
KVCACHE_LOOKUP hit=false route=/chat/large req_id=abc123
```
That aligns with the latency waterfall — lots of KV misses causing full re-encode.

Priya Rao: added a temporary panel to the perf dashboard: "kv_evictions / token latency (per-host)". dashboard-bot posted the link and updated the dashboard version. dashboard-bot: Updated dashboard ux/perf_kv_evictions (v12) - panels added: kv_evictions_heatmap, evict_vs_token_latency.

dashboard-bot: New panel URLs: https://graf.redwood.internal/d/uxr3k/perf_kv_evictions?panelId=7 and https://graf.redwood.internal/d/uxr3k/perf_kv_evictions?panelId=8 :white_check_mark:

Mateo Alvarez: quick repro script I ran in canary:```bash
# simulate chat/large with varied prefix lengths
for L in 16 64 128 256; do
  seq 1 50 | xargs -P4 -I{} curl -s -X POST https://canary-api.internal/generate -d '{"route":"/chat/large","prefix_len":'$L'}'
done
```

Sam Nguyen: thanks. plan: roll kernel-selector rollback on canary (20% traffic) and run the repro for 30m, watch kv_evictions & sm_occupancy. If stable, stage rollout to the rest. Priya — can you keep the kv_evictions panel on 30s refresh for the next 6h?

Priya Rao: will do. set to 30s and pinned to the ENG_RUNTIME dashboard group. also added an alert rule: kv_evictions_rate > 50/min AND token_p50 > 0.12s for 5m -> page oncall.

oncall-bot: Alert rule created: EVCT-LAT-REGRESSION. Oncall: @Lee Ortega will be paged for priority-2 alerts.

Lee Ortega: i'm on it. starting the canary rollback now and will post logs. Expect a 3–5 minute window for kube rolling.

Sam Nguyen: appreciate it. If the rollback fixes it, we'll keep the kernel-selector change off until we can add a KV-aware cost term to the selector. That selector needs to consider predicted working set size and kv-hit probability, not just raw kernel throughput.

Mateo Alvarez: Agreed. I can prototype a small estimator: use recent prefix length distribution + route model to estimate KV hit ratio and penalize kernels that increase working set. Will spike a PR for an experimental flag.

Sam Nguyen: perfect. keep messages short in this thread so we have a clean audit trail.

title: #incidents: 1852009876
preview:
PagerDuty: Incident triggered: MET-INGEST-ANOMALY (severity: P1)
Service: metering-ingest
Alert: token_count delta > 20% vs baseline for 15m
Link: https://pagerduty.example/INC12345

incident-bot: page fired to oncall (priaya_oncall). Runbook suggested: metering ingestion restart + backfill.

Priya Rao: ack. I'm looking — seeing spike in duplicate token counts from kafka-partition-4. Pulling logs and consumer offsets.

Mateo Alvarez: I can help with consumer side. Any chance aggregator job is stuck? Last commit offset seen 1245789.

Priya Rao: tail of aggregator logs:
```
2026-03-14T02:45:22Z ERROR aggregator failed to flush batch: timeout
2026-03-14T02:45:22Z WARN duplicate token detected count=3421 partition=4
2026-03-14T02:45:23Z INFO retrying batch flush
```

Jun Park: escalating to SRE lead. Priya, have you tried a rolling restart of the aggregator pods `metering-agg-prod`? Could be KV cache growth or stuck IO.

Priya Rao: planning restart now. Doing `kubectl rollout restart deployment/metering-agg-prod` (rolling). Will monitor lag.

BillingBot: ALERT: 43 customer invoices currently queued waiting on token reconciliation. If pipeline stays stalled invoices may be delayed.

Sophie (CS): flagged top customers and opened support threads. Prep notification template for possible invoice delay.

Mateo Alvarez: restart triggered, pods cycling. Also temporarily scaling replicas to 6 to help drain backlog.

Priya Rao: pods back up. Consumer lag dropped from ~150k -> ~20k offsets. Token delta reduced to ~3%. Continuing to monitor for 10m.

incident-bot: PagerDuty: incident acknowledged by Priya Rao (priaya_oncall). Escalation paused.

Jun Park: good mitigation. Next step: re-commit stuck offsets or run backfill consumer. I'll prep the re-commit job in 10m in case backlog doesn't finish.

Priya Rao: starting re-commit job `metering-recommit-2026-03-14` now with `--max-batch=2000` to avoid big spikes.

BillingBot: Update: re-commit started. Estimated throughput ~2k tokens/sec; estimated catch-up time ~2.5 hours.

Sophie (CS): updating customers with ETA ~3h, message drafted: "We are processing a backlog which may delay invoices by up to 3 hours — engineering is actively remediating." :phone:

Priya Rao: handing off to night oncall (Jun Park) at 04:00 UTC. Leaving runbook notes and dashboards in the thread.

Jun Park: ack handoff. I'll watch the re-commit and escalate to Eng Infra if throughput drops. Good job team :thumbsup:

PagerDuty: Status -> monitoring. If metrics (token delta, consumer lag) stay within thresholds for 30m we'll resolve the incident.

-- end thread

title: #sales: 1839123456
preview:
Emily Harper: Heads-up — Fathom Labs (enterprise AI analytics) asked for a hosted pilot concession. They want a temporary per-token rate for embeddings due to very high volume.
Raj Singh: How big is "very high"? ballpark numbers pls.
Emily Harper: Rough estimate from kickoff: 50M embeddings over 90 days. Each embedding ~ 512 tokens -> ~25.6B tokens.
Mateo Alvarez: whoa that's nontrivial for hosted. Are they insisting on hosted or open to Dedicated?
Emily Harper: They prefer hosted for speed of iteration. Dedicated would be heavier ops + procurement, they said pilot needs to be low friction.
Raj Singh: What's our standard hosted embedding rate today?
Priya Chen: Standard: $0.10 per 1K tokens for embeddings (public rate card). Min monthly commit kicks in at $5k for pilot accounts by policy.
Emily Harper: Their ask: waive the $5k min for the 90-day pilot and give a one-time discounted per-token rate of $0.03 per 1K tokens for embeddings only.
Mateo Alvarez: Technical note: at 25.6B tokens, cost at standard would be ~$2,560 (if $0.10 per 1K -> $0.00010 per token -> 25.6B * 0.0001 = $2.56M) wait double-check math.
Raj Singh: quick calc: $0.10/1K = $0.0001/token. 25.6B tokens * $0.0001 = $2,560,000. yep.
Priya Chen: That volume would normally be handled as a committed deal or Dedicated. The ask to drop to $0.03/1K would be a 70% discount and materially below acceptable floors.
Raj Singh: Can we offer a structured alternative? e.g., temporary tiering: first 100M tokens at $0.03/1K, remaining at $0.08/1K, and require a $50k pilot deposit (refundable / creditable)?
Priya Chen: Finance guidance: we can approve up to a 25% discount off list without VP sign-off for pilots. Anything deeper needs Deal Desk + revenue ops + VP approval. Also we cannot waive min entirely for this scale without a refundable deposit or commitment.
Emily Harper: Fathom is pushing back on deposit. They want to avoid upfront money for procurement reasons. Any levers?
Mateo Alvarez: We can propose usage caps + automatic throttles to limit spend risk, and prefix KV caching to substantially reduce token usage for embeddings in practice. But that doesn't replace economics.
Raj Singh: I'll open a Deal Desk request. Can you send a concise ask format? copy/paste for the form: customer, pilot length, est tokens, requested rate, requested waive min, impact to revenue, rep rec.
Emily Harper: copy: "Fathom Labs — 90d pilot — est 25.6B tokens — requested embeddings rate $0.03/1K — waive $5k min — rep rec: offer tiered concession: $0.03/1K first 200M tokens, $0.07/1K next 1B, $0.09/1K thereafter + $50k refundable pilot deposit or auto-convert to 3mo commit at $0.07/1K."
Raj Singh: perfect. submitting now. @deal-desk-bot can you create a thread and route to VP RevOps?
deal-desk-bot: Request created — DD-38947. Routing to @Priya Chen and @VP-RevOps. Please attach the cost model and customer LOE.
Priya Chen: I'll review DD-38947. Preliminary: we like the tiered approach + refundable deposit. If customer absolutely refuses deposit, we could accept a smaller deposit but then need executive approval and legal to sign an Addendum limiting liability.
Emily Harper: I'll push the tiered + refundable deposit option with Fathom and ask if they will auto-convert to a short commit instead of deposit.
Mateo Alvarez: FYI — if they convert to a committed volume we can offer service-level routing and pre-warmed caches to keep latency predictable. Mention that to them.
Raj Singh: Thanks all. I'll follow up after DD decisions. :thumbsup:
text:
Emily Harper: Heads-up — Fathom Labs (enterprise AI analytics) asked for a hosted pilot concession. They want a temporary per-token rate for embeddings due to very high volume.
Raj Singh: How big is "very high"? ballpark numbers pls.
Emily Harper: Rough estimate from kickoff: 50M embeddings over 90 days. Each embedding ~ 512 tokens -> ~25.6B tokens.
Mateo Alvarez: whoa that's nontrivial for hosted. Are they insisting on hosted or open to Dedicated?
Emily Harper: They prefer hosted for speed of iteration. Dedicated would be heavier ops + procurement, they said pilot needs to be low friction.
Raj Singh: What's our standard hosted embedding rate today?
Priya Chen: Standard: $0.10 per 1K tokens for embeddings (public rate card). Min monthly commit kicks in at $5k for pilot accounts by policy.
Emily Harper: Their ask: waive the $5k min for the 90-day pilot and give a one-time discounted per-token rate of $0.03 per 1K tokens for embeddings only.
Mateo Alvarez: Technical note: at 25.6B tokens, cost at standard would be ~$2,560 (if $0.10 per 1K -> $0.00010 per token -> 25.6B * 0.0001 = $2.56M) wait double-check math.
Raj Singh: quick calc: $0.10/1K = $0.0001/token. 25.6B tokens * $0.0001 = $2,560,000. yep.
Priya Chen: That volume would normally be handled as a committed deal or Dedicated. The ask to drop to $0.03/1K would be a 70% discount and materially below acceptable floors.

title: #incidents: 1799001234
preview:
incident-bot: PagerDuty alert triggered -> PROD auth.token.validate error rate spike. Service: auth-signing. Severity: P1. page sent to on-call: Mateo Alvarez. link: https://pagerduty.example/incidents/PD-9981

pagerduty-bot: ALERT: auth.token.validate failures >= 15% over 5m (from 0.4% baseline). Recent deploy: auth-service@2026-03-14-rc3. Error types: 401 (invalid token) and 500 (signing errors). Escalation policy: primary -> secondary after 5m.

Mateo Alvarez: acked. I'm on it — looking at traces and logs now. ETA 15m.

Mateo Alvarez: quick check — are we seeing region skew? I see a lot of failures from eu-west-1 customers.

Priya Nair: confirming — Cloudwatch shows spike starting +00:03 after the rc3 deploy. Affected endpoints: /v1/token/validate and /v1/auth/challenge. Impact: ~12 customers with elevated 401s, 3 enterprise SSO sessions failing.

Carlos Ruiz: I rolled rc3 which included JWKS cache sharding change + a small refactor to the signing client. Could be JWKS KID ordering change. Rolling back if needed.

Alice Chen: paging support: holding off external comms until we know scope. internal playbook says notify customers if >10% errors or if any enterprise SSO broken.

Mateo Alvarez: grabbing auth logs. snippet: ```
ERROR auth.signing: kid mismatch: token.kid=key_abc123 not found in JWKS
stack=auth.service.validate() request_id=req-0a1b2c
```

Mateo Alvarez: also seeing 500s from signing-service: ```
WARN signing.client: failed to sign payload: crypto: unknown key format
trace=... instance=signing-02
```

Priya Nair: PagerDuty escalation: secondary on-call (Carlos Ruiz) paged (escalation after 5m).

Carlos Ruiz: oncall here — joining. I suspect rc3 changed the key rotation handshake; keys may be published with a new KID ordering and consumers are using cached JWKS shards. Start plan: 1) revert rc3 to rc2 on auth-service 2) flush JWKS cache in edge caches 3) enable fallback to legacy key set for 10m.

Mateo Alvarez: running rollback command on staging then will do prod rollback if green. Command I'll run: ```
kubectl set image deploy/auth-service auth-service=ghcr.io/redwood/auth-service:2026-03-14-rc2 -n prod
```

Alice Chen: Support: list of potentially impacted customers (from telemetry): AcmeCorp, BrightLoan, Healthlytics, DataFlow Labs. Prepared templated note but waiting.

Pagerduty-bot: Incident escalated to secondary (Carlos Ruiz) at +00:05. Acknowledgement recorded.

Priya Nair: Mateo — handing off monitoring and runbook ops to me if you need short break. I'm available for on-call handoff in 10m.

Mateo Alvarez: handing to Priya in 10. I've started rollback on one AZ to validate. Observability: error rate dropped from 18% -> 5% on that AZ after rollback.

Carlos Ruiz: I'm pushing a fast rollback across all pods now. Also issuing JWKS cache flush to CDN and API gateway: ```
curl -X POST https://internal-admin.gateway/clear-jwks-cache -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN"
```

Support Bot: note created: INC-2026-03-14-9981, owners: Mateo, Carlos, Priya. Triage doc: https://redwood.slack/triage/INC-2026-03-14-9981 (private).

Priya Nair: after rollback + cache flush we're seeing validation errors fall to ~0.6%. Still above baseline but trending down. Running a gradual promote of rc2.

Mateo Alvarez: re-running validation load tests against EU region — tokens validating correctly now in Canary.

Carlos Ruiz: double-checked JWKS publication endpoint. There was a race where new key metadata published before signature rollout completed. Added short sleep in publisher in a follow-up patch; won't deploy now — will hold until postmortem.

Alice Chen: heads-up to account execs for the 3 enterprise customers with SSO outages. Draft message: "We're investigating an authentication validation issue affecting SSO logins for a small number of customers; engineers have rolled back a recent deploy and we're validating fixes. We'll update in 30 minutes." OK to send once we confirm stable? :thinking:

Priya Nair: yes hold until error rates <1% and enterprise test logins succeed.

Pagerduty-bot: Incident status: mitigation in progress. Next escalation window: +10m.

Mateo Alvarez: metrics now: auth.token.validate error rate 0.5% global, eu-west1 0.9% — trending down. Latency normal. No sign of data loss.

Carlos Ruiz: prod rollback completed at +00:27. JWKS cache flush issued. I ran sanity check: fetching JWKS and validating sample tokens succeeded. command used: ```
python tools/jwt_check.py --jwks https://jwks.prod.redwood/api/keys --token samples/ok.jwt
```

Priya Nair: Confirming: enterprise SSO smoke tests passed for AcmeCorp and Healthlytics. Support: please send customer update per template.

Alice Chen: sent to account execs and queued CS outreach for impacted customers. Template posted to channel. :email:

Mateo Alvarez: monitoring for 30m. If stable, we'll close and kick off a postmortem.

Carlos Ruiz: root cause hypothesis: JWKS publication race + small refactor changed KID ordering which broke some cached consumers. Mitigation: rollback + cache flush + temporary fallback to legacy keyset. Longer term: add publishing handshake + deployment gating and monitoring for key mismatches.

Support Bot: customer impact summary: ~12 customers experienced increased 401s for ~20-30m window; 3 enterprise SSO sessions fully degraded for ~18m. No data exfiltration.

Priya Nair: marking incident as resolved in PagerDuty. Mateo — handing back to you for postmortem.

Mateo Alvarez: thanks — will draft postmortem with timeline, RCA, and action items. Assigning action owners: Carlos for publisher fix, Priya for runbook update, Alice for customer comms template.

incident-bot: PagerDuty incident PD-9981 marked resolved by Priya Nair.

Mateo Alvarez: closing thread here. Good ops team. Postmortem draft link: https://docs.redwood/postmortems/PD-9981-draft (will circulate).

Alice Chen: adding a note to the change freeze checklist for JWKS-related deploys.

Carlos Ruiz: will open a follow-up PR with gating tests.

Support Bot: incident closed.

title: #eng-platform: 1795502048
preview:
Alice Chen: Hey team — can someone :eyes: this PR? https://github.com/redwood-inference/go-sdk/pull/814
Summary: unify client timeout handling between REST/streaming toolcall flows. Fixes sporadic goroutine leaks when default timeout was 0. Non-breaking in intent but changes implicit default behavior for timeouts.
CI: failing flaky test `TestStreamCancel` on linux-1
Asking for 2 approvers + a quick check from infra for load test impact. Hoping to merge today if green.

Priya Desai: @Alice Chen I can take this in ~1h. Quick q: does this change default exported constructor signature? We have a few on-prem customers pinning to implicit 0-timeout behavior.

Ben Ortiz: quick skim — looks good. main comment: we should add a short note in changelog re: default timeout semantics. Also can you add a small integration snippet for streaming cancel in tests? e.g.
```
ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 250*time.Millisecond)
defer cancel()
client.Stream(req, ctx)
```

DevOps-Bot: github-actions/cicd — build #814.3 failed on linux-1: TestStreamCancel flake. Retry queued.

Mateo Alvarez: gave it a pass :+1: left one nit about error wrapping. won't block. main behavior looks safe. @Priya if you're ok with default change I'd approve as well.

Linh Park: @release-manager FYI — might affect Dedicated customers with custom autoscaler timeouts. Recommend short rollback window if we see regressions.

Priya Desai: saw Ben's snippet, makes sense. I'll run the integration locally and post results in ~30m.

Alice Chen: thanks all. I'll re-run CI after Priya's local test, then request merge. tagging @ops to watch for early traffic blips.

Ops-Bot: ops-alerts — noted; will monitor p99 latency for go-sdk routes during rollout.

Priya Desai: local run passed w/ new test snippet, can't repro flaky. CI retriggered by Alice — now green.

Mateo Alvarez: approved in GH. :tada:

Alice Chen: merging after squash. Thanks everyone!

title: #new-hires: 1791112223
preview:
Jordan Park: Hey team — Jordan here, first day on infra. Trying to create a test VM in asia-southeast1 (project: redwood-analytics) and it fails with `PermissionDenied: compute.subnetworks.create`. I think I also don't have billing linked. Who do I ping to get billing attached + the right IAM for networking?

Lina Gomez: welcome Jordan! :wave: pinging infra folks. can you paste the exact error and the project id?

Jordan Park: error is `PermissionDenied: The caller does not have permission to perform compute.subnetworks.create on project redwood-analytics (or it may be missing BillingEnabled).` project id is `redwood-analytics` (same). quick repro: `gcloud compute networks subnets create test-subnet --network default --region asia-southeast1`

Raj Patel: two things likely: 1) billing not linked -> some APIs blocked, 2) missing IAM (roles/compute.networkAdmin or a narrower custom role). Also check quota in asia-southeast1 for CPUs.

Mateo Alvarez: I can link billing if you confirm which billing account (we have `billing-prod-001` and `billing-sandbox-002`). For new hires we usually attach sandbox. Jordan — want sandbox?

Jordan Park: sandbox is fine for the demo. thanks Mateo.

Lina Gomez: Jordan, I'll DM you the exact minimal roles we grant for sandbox dev: `roles/compute.instanceAdmin.v1`, `roles/compute.networkAdmin` and `roles/iam.serviceAccountUser` for the SA you use. If you only need to create one subnet + VM, networkAdmin + instanceAdmin is enough.

Raj Patel: quota check quick command:```bash
gcloud compute regions describe asia-southeast1 --project=redwood-analytics
```look at `quotas` block. If CPUs are at 0 you need to request increase (or use a different region).

Jordan Park: ran that, CPU quota shows 0/0 for `CPUS` (lol). Will request increase but can Mateo attach billing now so APIs actually enable?

Mateo Alvarez: done — linked `billing-sandbox-002` to `redwood-analytics`. ops-bot will create a ticket for tracking.

ops-bot: Ticket created: INFRA-3378 — "Link billing to redwood-analytics and grant sandbox dev IAM" — assigned to Mateo.

Lina Gomez: Mateo — once billing linked, Jordan should retry. If still blocked, I'll create a quick service account with the roles and add you. Jordan — here's the command to grant role (run by an admin):```bash
gcloud projects add-iam-policy-binding redwood-analytics --member=\"user:jordan.park@redwood.com\" --role=\"roles/compute.networkAdmin\"
```

Jordan Park: re-ran the subnet create after billing link and it worked! :tada: Thanks all. I'll file a quick onboarding note on where to request quota increases and who to ping.

Lina Gomez: awesome — dropping the quota request doc link here for future hires: https://redwood.internal/docs/cloud/quota-requests (internal).

Mateo Alvarez: welcome again Jordan — shout if you need Dedicated projects or help migrating demos later.

Jordan Park: will do, thanks! :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1789801234
preview:
incident-bot: [ALERT] smart-routing.policy_anomaly — cost_based_router pushed unexpected traffic to 'redwood-lite-quant' (low-cost variant). Quality deltas above threshold for multiple customers. Paging @Aj Patel (oncall).
Maria Chen: on it, looking at routing logs now. seeing a big spike at 09:05 UTC where weight for redwood-lite-quant jumps from ~2% -> 18% for us-east-1.
Aj Patel: ack. I'm up, pulling metrics. can you post the exact policy snapshot?
Maria Chen: grabbing it. `redwoodctl policies get cost_based_router --revision=2026-04-05T09:04Z`
Maria Chen: ```json
{
  "id": "cost_based_router",
  "version": "rev-87",
  "rules": [
    {"condition": "latency < 100ms", "score": 0.1},
    {"condition": "cost_score <= 0.25", "action": "prefer_low_cost_variant"}
  ]
}
```
Maria Chen: note the `cost_score <= 0.25` looks wrong — should be 0.025 based on our config template. that explains the aggressive shift.
Aj Patel: ugh, that's a decimal misplace. who applied the config? any recent rollouts?
Dana Rivera: I pushed rev-87 as part of the economy routing tweak this morning (small change), didn't realize cost thresholds were rescaled. i'm here.
Priya Singh: copying SLO/QA checks — we see downstream evaluation metrics: reference BLEU/QA-like metric dropped from 0.92 -> 0.76 for some eval sets after the shift. Support has started seeing churn in a few customer sessions.
Mateo Alvarez: timeline: 09:03 stable -> 09:05 policy revision applied -> 09:06 metric delta starts -> 09:08 error reports escalate. We should assume ~18% of traffic hit redwood-lite-quant between 09:05-09:15.
Aj Patel: immediate mitigation plan: 1) pause the offending policy 2) force-route critical customers to verified variants 3) revert policy revision to rev-86 4) follow up with a canary + guardrails. executing step 1 now.
Aj Patel: running: `redwoodctl policies pause cost_based_router --reason "prod quality regression"`
ops-bot: policy cost_based_router paused by Aj Patel at 2026-04-05T09:11:25Z :white_check_mark:
Maria Chen: confirming pause reflected in router logs, beginning to drain traffic from redwood-lite-quant. current P50 latency unaffected but quality signals climbing back to baseline.
Aj Patel: forcing route overrides for top-10 paying customers while we revert: `redwoodctl route override --customer @AcmeCorp --model redwood-3b-v2 --duration 30m`
SupportBot: creating support notes and tagging tickets for affected customers. Initial count: 7 tickets flagged as "generation degradation". agents pinged.
Priya Singh: can we snapshot requests that went to redwood-lite-quant for quick QA? need to feed into eval harness.
Maria Chen: grabbing sample: `/var/log/router/samples/2026-04-05/09-05-09-15-redwood-lite-quant.log` — uploading to shared dir now.
Dana Rivera: rev-86 is stable. I'm applying `redwoodctl policies apply --file policies/rev-86.json --force` to revert.
ops-bot: policy apply started (rev-86) by Dana Rivera at 2026-04-05T09:14:02Z
ops-bot: policy apply succeeded at 2026-04-05T09:14:46Z :white_check_mark:
Maria Chen: traffic distribution returned to expected state within ~90s of revert. quality metrics trending to prior baseline. Support: please update customers with rollback note.
Aj Patel: switching to incident choreography: I'm handing off to Dana for follow-up/fuzzy QA + postmortem. Dana, you take it?
Dana Rivera: taking the handoff. I'll own the postmortem, immediate action items: 1) audit policy ingestion to catch unit-scaling mistakes 2) add a preapply validation that compares thresholds to typical ranges 3) create a canary rollout for policy changes 4) notify customers and patch KB.
Priya Singh: also recommend adding a quality-safety gate in the routing pipeline: if a candidate variant would reduce eval_score > 0.05 for a customer, block preference automatically.
Mateo Alvarez: agree. metric thresholds + automated rollback on delta exceeding X should be standard.
Aj Patel: notes for pager: no data-plane downtime, partial customer impact (degraded generations) for a subset of traffic, 7 active support tickets, no billing/exposed PII. escalation level: SEV-2.
Dana Rivera: creating incident ticket https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INF-4321 and will draft postmortem. assigning follow-ups: infra-validation (Priya), policy-canary (Mateo), docs/KB (SupportBot).
SupportBot: INF-4321 created by Dana Rivera. Linked 7 support tickets, labelled incident:cost-routing-low-quality.
Maria Chen: quick follow: please run a small retro with config authors. also let's add a simple linter in the policy authoring flow to reject cost thresholds > 0.05 for low-cost preference rules.
Aj Patel: thanks team. I'll stay available for any escalation. handing off oncall to Dana in ~10m. :handshake:
Dana Rivera: thanks Aj, picking up. will post timeline and RCA draft by EOD. :memo:
incident-bot: incident resolved (mitigated) - cost_based_router paused and reverted to rev-86. severity: SEV-2 -> monitoring.
Maria Chen: marking thread resolved for now. thumbs if we can close once postmortem is published. :thumbsup:

title: #eng-runtime: 1771234567
preview:
Omar Rahman: Heads up — after the kernel-selection change that prefers the new FP4-sparse matmul for short sequences, we saw a ~25% throughput drop and intermittent memory spikes on prod-gpu-pool-3 around 06:42 UTC. graph: https://grafana.internal/r/xyz :eyes:
Priya Desai: ugh. do we have profiler traces? is this isolated to NF4 vs FP4 or the kernel switch path?
Omar Rahman: I grabbed a quick pprof on one affected node. attaching snippet below. high memcpy counts and sudden allocations in kv_cache codepath.
Alice Chen: paste of profiler (truncated):
```
CPU: 0.8s total
memcpy: 320ms
kernel_fp4_sparse_matmul: 180ms
kv_cache_flush: 90ms
alloc: 250MB spikes at request start
```
Noticed frequent kv_cache->evict cycles at ~1.2s intervals when sequence length > 512.
Mateo Alvarez: that eviction pattern smells like batching heuristic holding onto KV pages until a full batch triggers, then we do a big memcpy to populate sparse kernel input format. FP4-sparse needs data layout transform which seems to be O(n) and allocating a temp buffer per request.
Sofia Li: infra-bot showed a platform rollout for the kernel selector at 06:30 UTC (deploy 8c9a7). infra-bot: "deploy: kernel-selector v2 -> rollout 10% -> prod-gpu-pool-3"
Priya Desai: can you share the exact reproducer command? i'll run a controlled sweep (seq len, batch size, kv hit rate)
Omar Rahman: repro linked — run on canary: 
```
./bench_infer --model rwd-llm-13b-nf4 --quant nf4-fp4-sparse --seq 1024 --concurrency 8 --kv=on --prefix_cache=enabled --tokens 20000
```
Note: switching quant to nf4-int8 removes the spike.
Alice Chen: dug into the kernel impl — when sparse kernel path chosen we call convert_to_sparse_layout() which allocates a temp tensor sized [batch, heads, seq, head_dim_quant] per request and doesn't reuse across requests. Previously dense kernels used a shared scratch allocator. That's the mem leak-ish behavior.
Mateo Alvarez: so two issues: 1) layout transformation allocation pattern (per-request scratch) and 2) batching heuristic causes accumulation of larger seq groups that hit the conversion more often. Both increase memcpy + alloc time.
Priya Desai: hypothesis: calibration step for FP4 quantizer emits per-tensor scale tables which force extra per-layer metadata and a small fallback path that uses host-side staging buffers. That combined with layout conversion increases host<->device transfers. Do we have traces for PCIe memcpy vs device memcpy?
Omar Rahman: yes — host memcpy dominated. device memcpy negligible. That aligns with staging buffers on host.
Sofia Li: immediate mitigation options: rollback kernel-selector rollout OR patch kernel to use shared scratch pool and enable fallback to int8 for seq>512. Which is lower risk?
Mateo Alvarez: patching kernel for scratch reuse is low-risk but needs careful testing (sync/parallelism). Fallback to int8 for long seq is safer and fast to flip via policy.
Priya Desai: agree — enable policy: if seq_len>512 or kv_cache_hit_rate>0.6, route to int8 kernel. Also add a flag to disable FP4-sparse layout transform temporary allocation. I'll open a small PR.
Omar Rahman: canary plan: Sofia — can you flip policy on prod-canary for 1 hour with traffic mirror and run repro? i'll watch p99 and allocation metrics.
Sofia Li: on it. will schedule at 07:15 UTC.
Alice Chen: i'll prototype scratch-pool patch in a branch (alice/fp4-scratch-pool) and add unit tests for concurrent requests. Might take 2 days.
Omar Rahman: action items summarized:
- Sofia: flip policy to fallback int8 for long seq on prod-canary at 07:15 UTC (Sofia)
- Priya: PR for routing policy + tests (Priya)
- Alice: implement shared scratch allocator + tests (Alice)
- Mateo: add batching heuristic telemetry to detect accumulation patterns (Mateo)
I'll own the rollout rollback if canary still spikes.
Priya Desai: FYI i'll also run a calibration sanity check — maybe our FP4 calibrator produced extreme scale values for some layers after last model import.
Omar Rahman: thanks all. please post short updates in thread. :thumbsup:
